news.title: Statins and blood pressure drugs changing health risks of obesity, study suggests
news.summary: New research shows obesity patients over 40 are managing blood pressure and cholesterol as well as healthy-weight peers, but younger adults remain at risk.

news.title: STAT+: The moment Anthropic convinced me it’s serious about science
news.summary: AI Prognosis: The release of Claude Science suggests big model makers are getting serious about real science, and not just flashy problems.

news.title: STAT+: Synthetic biology researchers think they’ve made a cell. Is it alive?
news.summary: Researchers have created what they say may count as the first synthetic cell, and have started a public benefit corporation to share the technology with other scientists.  It’s a piece…

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Anthropic drug development goals, Trump drug plan snags, and more
news.summary: Anthropic announced the availability of Claude Science, which optimizes its large language model for use in scientific labs and by drugmakers

news.title: STAT+: More signs that GLP-1s may help with peripheral artery disease
news.summary: Anthropic will begin developing drugs, BridgeBio pockets a billion, and more biotech news from The Readout

news.title: FDA says Zyn can market its pouches as safer than cigarettes
news.summary: The influence of the alcohol industry on policy, AI Claude for scientists, and more health news from Morning Rounds

news.title: STAT+: FDA approves Orca Bio’s T cell therapy for blood cancer patients
news.summary: The FDA approved a T-cell therapy from Orca Bio that reduces the risk of a debilitating immune reaction in patients with blood cancers undergoing stem cell transplants.

news.title: Opinion: What Ebola and Marburg are teaching us about the next pandemic
news.summary: “What is urgently needed is a shift from pathogen-specific diagnostics toward pathogen-agnostic diagnostic systems,” write Krutika Kuppalli and Placide Mbala.

news.title: Opinion: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore: How to improve young men’s mental health
news.summary: “We need to stop treating our young men and boys as problems to be solved, and start treating them as people to be invested in,” writes Gov. Wes Moore.

news.title: STAT+: The biotech scorecard for the third quarter: 15 stock-moving events to watch
news.summary: Here is STAT’s biotech scorecard, our regular ledger of stock-moving biotech events, for the third quarter.

news.title: STAT+: Booze schmooze: The alcohol industry, frazzled by headwinds, wields its power behind the scenes
news.summary: Demand for alcohol is declining. The industry is fighting harder and more creatively than ever against limits — and winning.

news.title: STAT+: AI company Anthropic announces it will begin developing drugs of its own
news.summary: AI giant Anthropic has already become a dominant player in technology and a household name for everyday users of artificial intelligence. Can it make drugs too?

news.title: Republican Tom Kean Jr. reveals depression diagnosis after his four-month absence from Congress
news.summary: Depression, the New Jersey congressman said, “is physical, it is emotional, and until you experience it yourself, it is difficult to fully understand how powerful this illness can be.”

news.title: STAT+: Anthropic releases Claude Science, a product aimed at researchers, the pharma industry
news.summary: Anthropic released Claude Science, an application that optimizes its large language model for scientists and, especially, those doing research at pharma companies.

news.title: Marburg outbreak is reported in Uganda, threatening to complicate Ebola response in region
news.summary: Sources confirm to STAT that Marburg cases have been detected in western Uganda, adding another viral hemorrhagic fever outbreak in central Africa.

news.title: CMS mulls tougher Medicare enrollment rules to combat fraud as part of 2027 home health payment rule
news.summary: CMS is proposing changes to provider enrollment provisions aimed at strengthening the agency's ability to recover improper payments and remove noncompliant providers and suppliers from Medicare.

news.title: CMS goes live with GLP-1 Bridge program for Part D beneficiaries
news.summary: The Trump administration has officially launched its new "bridge" model that brings GLP-1 coverage for weight loss to Medicare beneficiaries.

news.title: Trump administration withholds funds from New York's Medicaid fraud unit
news.summary: As part of its efforts to crack down on fraud in Medicaid, the Trump administration revealed that it will suspend funding for New York's state Medicaid Fraud Control Unit.

news.title: Zelis rolls out AI solution to help payers navigate No Surprises Act dispute process
news.summary: Healthcare technology company Zelis rolled out an AI-native solution to help health plans manage Independent Dispute Resolution complexity under the No Surprises Act.

news.title: Experity acquires Exdion Healthcare to accelerate on-demand care RCM automation
news.summary: The acquisition aims to accelerate RCM automation for on-demand care.

news.title: ACCESS Model: behavioral health edition
news.summary: A new payment model is designed to pay digital health companies based on outcomes in chronic condition management. But where does behavioral health fit? This episode explores that question with SonderMind and Headspace, two companies participating in the model's behavioral health track.

news.title: Saint Peter’s Healthcare System Expands Intelligent Hospital Room Initiative with hellocare.ai to Advance AI Assisted Patient Safety and Virtual Care
news.summary: hellocare.ai is expanding its partnership with Saint Peter's Healthcare System to deploy advanced intelligent hospital room capabilities across the organization.

news.title: Evernorth unveils new AI-powered specialty pharmacy program, Pharmacy Forward
news.summary: Evernorth has unveiled a new specialty pharmacy program that leans on AI to support a better experience for patients with complex conditions.

news.title: 26 states sue CMS over final Medicaid work requirements rule
news.summary: A coalition of 26 states is suing the Trump administration over its rollout of Medicaid work requirements.

news.title: Startup Queue lands $12.6M to launch autonomous robotic pharmacy kiosks
news.summary: Startup Queue emerged from stealth to launch what it calls the first fully autonomous robotic pharmacy in the form of prescription medication kiosks.

news.title: Carbon Health agrees to revise contracts with its California clinics, pay penalties
news.summary: The primary care chain's settlement agreement follows a California DOJ investigation of its advertising and billing practices, as well as contracts that allegedly gave the company de facto control over its physician-owned clinics.

news.title: AI playing a major role in consumers' healthcare decision-making, survey finds
news.summary: Patients are taking charge of their healthcare with artificial intelligence and other solutions, with 58% of U.S. patients researching symptoms before deciding whether to book an appointment, a survey found.

news.title: Can We Outrun Cancer's Evolution? Why BrYet Is Targeting Cancer-Specific Phenotypes
news.summary: Genetic instability makes metastatic cancer largely incurable. BrYet aims to work around it with a new approach targeting cancer-specific phenotypes.

news.title: Insilico keeps partnership sprint apace in $600M biobucks collab with Takeda
news.summary: Insilico Medicine has signed a deal worth up to $600 million with Japanese Big Pharma Takeda to collaborate on discovery of assets across Takeda’s therapeutic areas of interest.

news.title: Merck halts ph. 2 Alzheimer’s trial after Neuphoria small molecule underwhelms
news.summary: Merck & Co. has nixed the phase 2 study of a potential Alzheimer’s disease medicine after the drug underwhelmed in an interim analysis, marking another blow for the pharma’s struggling partner Neuphoria Therapeutics.

news.title: Zimmer Biomet buys non-drug pain relief system from Pacira for up to $140M
news.summary: Pacira BioSciences offloaded its cold-therapy pain relief device to medtech orthopedics specialist Zimmer Biomet in a deal valued at up to $140 million.

news.title: Biotechs ‘in better position to avoid workforce reductions’ as layoffs continue to ease in Q2’26
news.summary: A sharp reduction in layoffs continued in the second quarter, extending a trend that started last fall.

news.title: Ipsen gets the Memo, penning €700M deal to buy rare disease biotech for clinical-stage asset
news.summary: Ipsen is on a roll this week, scooping up its second biotech in three days in order to firm up its pipeline with promising clinical-stage candidates.

news.title: Backed by top-tier VCs, stealthy biotech bags AlzeCure Alzheimer’s asset in $2.2B deal
news.summary: QuantumCell has licensed AlzeCure Pharma’s Alzheimer’s disease platform and lead asset for $12 million upfront, bagging a potentially symptom-relieving drug candidate that is on the cusp of phase 2.

news.title: Bain-backed bone specialist Angitia drops lead spinal fusion candidate
news.summary: Coming on the heels of a $130 million fundraising round just four months ago, bone-focused Angitia Biopharmaceuticals has pivoted away from its lead asset to structure its pipeline around two earlier-stage drug candidates.

news.title: FDA gene therapy regulator Vijay Kumar steps down from leadership role
news.summary: Vijay Kumar, the acting head of the FDA office that oversees new gene therapies, is stepping down from his role after a year in the job.

news.title: Abivax rebounds from stock drop with safety data for much-hyped colitis prospect
news.summary: Abivax has recovered from a dent in its share price after unveiling fresh safety data that appeared to ease investors’ concerns with its ulcerative colitis prospect.

news.title: China approved the world’s first solid tumor CAR-T therapy. When will the US follow?
news.summary: CAR-T cell therapy has flourished since it was first used in 2010, leading to seven approved medicines for different blood cancers. But adapting the powerful technique, which involves engineering a patient’s own T cells to fight cancer, to solid tumors has proven a vexing challenge.

news.title: Vistagen fails another phase 3 social anxiety trial but spies path to market
news.summary: Vistagen’s social anxiety disorder drug candidate has flunked another phase 3 trial. But, with a post hoc subpopulation analysis linking fasedienol to a nominally statistically significant improvement, the biotech plans to talk to the FDA about a path to approval built around a single future phase 3 study and confirmatory evidence from its broader PALISADE program.

news.title: AI进入下半场：模型不再稀缺，真正稀缺的是算力、场景和信任
news.summary: AI告别跑分时代

news.title: 真健康VS睿触机器人：国产穿刺机器人双雄，打响县城之战
news.summary: 得县城者得手术量，得手术量者得耗材复购率，最终方得“天下”。

news.title: 这家国产手术机器人今日IPO，市值超130亿
news.summary: 切入经皮穿刺手术机器人这一细分赛道。

papers.title: BioInsight: Multi-Agent Orchestration for Interactive Biomedical Knowledge Discovery
papers.summary: Biomedical researchers increasingly use AI-generated analyses and reports to interpret protein-level signals, but static outputs are often insufficient for research decision-making, where users need to inspect evidence, assess uncertainty, compare mechanisms, and refine hypotheses. We present BioInsight, a multi-agent system that moves from static biomedical report generation to interactive evidence-centered interactive interface generation. Given a disease name, a protein association table, and optional cohort metadata, BioInsight organizes disease-specific evidence through typed intermediate

papers.title: AtomiMed: Hierarchical Atomic Fact-Checking for Universal Clinical-Aware Medical Report Evaluation
papers.summary: Traditional metrics for Medical Report Generation (MRG) predominantly rely on surface-level n-gram overlap, which fails to capture clinical factual accuracy and often overlooks catastrophic diagnostic errors. We address this fundamental limitation by proposing AtomiMed, a universal, modality-agnostic evaluation framework that decomposes complex medical narratives into a standardized, multi-level hierarchy of Atomic Clinical Facts, encompassing Disease-level entities and Attribute-level descriptors, including location, morphology, and severity. By implementing an Agentic Cross-Verification loop

papers.title: RepoRescue: An Empirical Study of LLM Agents on Whole-Repository Compatibility Rescue
papers.summary: Open-source libraries and tools are widely reused, but compatibility maintenance is expensive. Once maintainers leave, useful repositories can stop working as runtimes and dependencies evolve. We study whether LLM agents can adapt old repositories to modern environments, a task we call compatibility rescue. Unlike bug repair, compatibility rescue starts from a repository that worked in its original environment but fails after ecosystem drift. RepoRescue gives agents only the repository and its failing modern environment; the agent must diagnose the failure, locate affected code, and produce a
